fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips clamping when clamp is True. It should be `if clamp:` to clamp when clamping is requested.

spec.expr: clamp == True or (clamp == False and True)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the branch that restricts y should be entered; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so clamping happens when clamp=False instead of True.
The then-branch of `if not clamp` should only execute when clamp is False; if clamp is True we should not be here.
y__2 (the clamped value) is only computed when `not clamp` is True, meaning clamping is applied when clamp=False — the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: not clamp or (min(y0,y1) <= __cbfl_result <= max(y0,y1) or abs(__cbfl_result - y0) < 1e-9 or abs(__cbfl_result - y1) < 1e-9)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the returned value should be within the y-range of the segment endpoints.
